findings.issue: LoginPage OAuth init failure leaves a stale invitation_token in sessionStorage, unlike the invitation accept flow and OAuth callback which both clear it. This creates asymmetric cleanup and can pollute a later invitation-complete fallback if the URL token is missing.
findings.remediation: Add clearInvitationToken() alongside clearVerifier() in the LoginPage handleOAuthLogin catch block.

findings.issue: React.StrictMode double-mount still allows the first (abandoned) redeemOAuth/acceptInvitation request to hit the network in OAuthCallbackPage and InvitationCompletePage. The AbortController only suppresses state updates after each await, so a single-use authorization code may be consumed by the ghost effect and the visible second effect can fail in development.
findings.remediation: Thread the AbortSignal into fetch/makeRequest (redeemOAuth, acceptInvitation) so outgoing requests are actually cancelled on cleanup, or guard each async boundary with the aborted signal before issuing the request and before setting state.

findings.issue: The decisions ledger acceptance evidence reports the remote-frontend suite as 25 files and 114 tests, but the current run is 133 tests. Stale counts make the ledger a less trustworthy gate record.
findings.remediation: Re-run the listed gate commands and update the test counts in the acceptance evidence to match the current suite (25 files, 133 tests).

findings.issue: AppRouter.tsx imports from @/pkce twice on separate lines, leaving a minor import duplication smell.
findings.remediation: Consolidate the two @/pkce imports into a single import statement.
